description: Cross-sector baseline compliance controls for Canada v1. Designed to be applied together with sector-specific overlays. Rules are evidence-driven and deterministic; absence of evidence does not by itself constitute legal non-compliance.

metadata.scope: Canada baseline controls for document/document-set review. Includes federal privacy, cybersecurity, AML/ATF, electronic marketing, consumer protection, and evidence controls. Provincial/territorial obligations may also apply and require human review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official Canadian federal/provincial/territorial legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and FINTRAC guidance
Baseline Cyber Security Controls for Small and Medium Organizations

rules.title: Privacy notice or personal-information handling disclosure evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for PIPEDA-style privacy notice, privacy policy, data protection notice, or personal-information handling language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consent, purpose, and access/correction evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for evidence of consent, purpose specification, access, correction, or withdrawal language related to personal information.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Cybersecurity incident response and access-control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for incident response, patching, authentication, access control, backup, or encryption evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security baseline controls

rules.title: CASL consent, sender identification, and unsubscribe evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks commercial electronic message documents for consent, identification/contact information, and unsubscribe mechanism evidence.

rules.title: AML/ATF compliance program or client-identification evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for FINTRAC-style compliance program, client identification, record keeping, reporting, or risk assessment evidence.

rules.title: False or misleading representation control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for truthful advertising, substantiation, disclosure, or anti-misrepresentation controls.
